steps.title: Repeat until they get it right

narration.default: Last tutorial you nested an if inside an else to give a second chance. But what if you want to keep asking forever, until the kid guesses right? Copying the same code over and over wouldn't scale. Today you'll meet the while loop — Python's way of saying "keep doing this as long as a condition is true". In Scratch this is the "repeat until" block. In Python it's one keyword: while.

Type the opening:

print("Guess the number!")

number = 5

guess = int(input("Enter your guess: "))

Same three lines you've used twice already — title, secret, first guess. The interesting bit comes next.

steps.title: Give a hint inside the loop
Ask for a new guess — still inside the loop

narration.default:
Still indented inside the while, type:

    guess = int(input("Try again: "))

This is critical — you MUST change guess somewhere inside the loop. Otherwise guess stays the same number it was before the loop started, guess != number stays true forever, and the loop never ends. That's called an infinite loop — a real bug you'll hit more than once.

Go back to the very left edge (no indent) and type:

print("Correct!")

This line is OUTSIDE the while — Python only runs it after the loop has finished. And the loop only finishes when guess == number. So by the time this line runs, the kid has guessed right. Notice how the indentation tells the whole story of what runs when.

Click Run. Start far away — type 1. Terminal: Too low. Try 9 — Too high. Try 3 — Too low. Try 6 — Too high. Try 5 — Correct! The loop ran four times and each time re-asked for a guess. The moment your guess matched, the loop stopped and the celebration printed.

Two dangers with while. One: forgetting to update the variable inside the loop, so the condition never changes — infinite loop. Two: what if the kid just wants to QUIT without guessing? Right now the only way out is to guess correctly. In the next tutorial you'll learn break — a way to pop out of a loop early, even if the condition is still true.
